Reach out to us for assistance: Email : USD@phoenix.edu Chat : Viva Engage (formerly Yammer) Slack: #help-university_service_desk Phone : 602-714-2714 Coverage : Mon-Fri: 5 a.m. - 5 p.m. AZ Time. Need to submit a Request? Visit us on the Service Portal . 👀 Featured articles Introduction to the new Service Portal: Start Here! Password Management with Microsoft Azure AD (SSPR) Local Admin Requests in Fischer Identity Ricoh Digital Mail Using live chat to request assistance 🕑 Recently updated page 5 true

Let's get started on backing up your primary working folders (Desktop, Documents, and Pictures) to the Cloud. This will not only ensure your files are safe but will also automatically sync any future files you add or edit. Plus, if you ever upgrade your computer, restoring your files will be a breeze! Before we start, make sure you are logged into OneDrive using your UOP employee email account. This is a crucial step as it allows your files to be securely backed up and synced. Instructions From the Taskbar (bottom right), navigate by selecting: OneDrive icon > Help & Settings > Settings > Backup > Manage backup. (if OneDrive is missing, use Windows Search to find it, then open and login with your email address) Confirm that all 3 of the folders (Desktop, Documents, Pictures) are selected then choose Start backup Close the next window or choose to monitor the backup progress. The backup is complete when you see "Up to date" when hovering over the OneDrive icon. More information from Microsoft along with a one-minute video: Backup with OneDrive Related articles The content by label feature displays related articles automatically, based on labels you choose. To edit options for this feature, select the placeholder below and tap the pencil icon. false 5 com.atlassian.confluence.content.render.xhtml.model.resource.identifiers.SpaceResourceIdentifier@14966 modified false true page label in ( "onedrive" , "365" ) and type = "page" and space = "USD" OneDrive 365 true Related issues

Purpose: The purpose of this SOP is to guide users on how to use their UoPX email address as their Apple ID. This ensures that users can take advantage of Single Sign-On (SSO) and synchronize their UoPX domain with their Apple account.

Scope: This SOP covers the process of using a UoPX email address as an Apple ID. It is applicable to all employees who wish to use their UoPX email for their Apple account.

Prerequisites:

  • None

Responsibilities:

  • Staff: Follow the steps to use their UoPX email as their Apple ID.

  • USD: Assist users with any issues related to using their UoPX email as their Apple ID.

Understand the Pros and Cons:

Pros:

  • Automatically keep up to date with your Apple ID and your UoPX Windows passwords.

  • No need to memorize another password to access apps.

  • Get a copy of approved UoPX apps to your phone (Outlook, Excel, Word, etc.).

Cons:

  • You cannot purchase apps not sent to your device.

Note: There is an easy workaround; use a different Apple ID for your iTunes account.

Steps to use a different Apple ID for the iTunes store:

Step 1: Search for the “iTunes Store” app on your iOS device.
Step 2: Launch the app.
Step 3: Scroll to the very bottom of the app.
Step 4: Tap on your Apple ID and select “Sign Out.”
Step 5: Log into the iTunes Store with a new Apple ID.

References:

  • None

", "metadata": {"title": "Can I use my UoPX Email as my Apple ID?", "space": "", "url": ""}, "format": "confluence_html", "version": "1.0"}

Problem/Issue: Users need to upgrade their Genesys Workspace Desktop Edition (WDE) and SIP Endpoint to the latest version.

Environment:

  • Genesys Workspace Desktop Edition (WDE)

  • Company Portal

  • Access to the Corp network or VPN is required

Symptoms:

  • Difficulty in upgrading Genesys WDE and its components.

  • Issues with installation and configuration

Cause: N/A

Resolution:

  1. Navigate to Company Portal:

    • Open the Company Portal from the Windows Start menu or search for it.

    • Click the Settings Cog in the lower left corner of the Company Portal.

  2. Hang Up and Exit Genesys:

    • Hang up any active calls, disposition if needed, and exit Genesys.

    • Note: The installation time for Genesys will be at least 10-15 minutes depending on internet speeds.

  3. Sync Company Portal:

    • Click the Sync button at the top of the page.

    • The sync process will start and display "Syncing…" and disappear when completed.

    • If the sync takes more than 10 minutes, reboot your computer and try again, then move on to the next step.

  4. Search for Genesys:

    • Once the sync completes, search for "Genesys" from the search bar in the left pane.

    • Click on "Genesys Workspace Desktop Ed. (WDE) & SIP Endpoint – 2023" and then click on "Install."

    • STOP: The Genesys install is not complete until you see "Installed" in the Company Portal and receive an "Installed Successfully" notification.

  5. Install Additional Components:

    • Once WDE and SIP are installed, proceed to install Genesys Agent Scripting and/or Co-Browse as needed.

  6. Reboot Your Computer:

    • Click the Windows/Start button, select the power icon, and then choose "Restart."

  7. Launch Genesys:

    • Launch the new Genesys app from the Start menu (port/host/config information should be retained).

    • Note: 100% is not an indicator that the install is completed. Please wait for the notification before launching Workspace Desktop.

  8. Disposition Calls and Screen Sharing:

    • If you need to disposition calls, install Agent Scripting.

    • If you need to screen share from Genesys, install Co-Browse.

    • Ensure the installation is completed before moving on.

  9. Taskbar Shortcut:

    • If there was a pre-existing taskbar shortcut for Genesys, delete it and re-create it from the Start menu: right-click (WDE) > More > Pin to taskbar.

  10. Login Information:

    • Application Name: WDE_SIP_NEW

    • Host/Configuration: genesysconfigproxy.apollogrp.edu

    • Port: 2020

Workaround: N/A

Additional Notes:

  • Ensure to follow the steps accurately to avoid any issues with the upgrade process.

  • Ensure VPN is connected properly, as it is required for use of Genesys.

  • For further assistance, contact USD.

", "metadata": {"title": "Genesys Upgrade", "space": "", "url": ""}, "format": "confluence_html", "version": "1.0"}

Problem/Issue: Users may find that their Outlook view has changed unexpectedly, causing confusion and difficulty in navigating their emails. This article addresses how to reset the view in Outlook to its default settings. Environment: Microsoft Outlook (any version) Windows operating system (Windows 10, Windows 11) Applicable to both desktop and web versions of Outlook Symptoms: Email columns are not displayed as expected The layout of the inbox or other folders is different from the default view Difficulty in finding or organizing emails due to the changed view Cause: The view settings in Outlook may have been altered due to user customization, software updates, or accidental changes. Resolution: Open Outlook: Launch the Outlook application on your computer. Navigate to the View Tab: In Outlook, at the top, switch to the "View" tab. Click on the 3 dots (…) and select customize. Reset the View: in the bottom left click the "Reset all changes" button. Click on “Save” Alternative Method Using Command: Press Windows + R to open the Run dialog box. Type outlook.exe /cleanviews and press Enter. This command will launch Outlook and reset all custom views to the default settings. Launch Outlook in Safe Mode: If the above methods do not work, try launching Outlook in Safe Mode. Press Windows + R to open the Run dialog box. Type outlook.exe /safe and press Enter. In the "Choose Profile" prompt, select your default email profile and click "OK." Navigate to the "View" tab and click "Reset View." Workaround: If the issue persists, consider creating a new Outlook profile: Open Control Panel and select "Mail." Click "Show Profiles" and then "Add" to create a new profile. Follow the prompts to set up your email account in the new profile. Additional Notes: Regularly check for updates to ensure the best performance. Disabling add-ins can help identify if any are causing the view issues. Navigate to File > Options > Add-Ins, and manage your add-ins from there.

Problem/Issue: Users are unable to upload PDF files and other acceptable formats to EXP/Kofax due to the system not recognizing the file extensions. Environment: Software: EXP/Kofax Operating System: Windows 10 or higher User Profile Path: C:\Users\userid\Documentum Symptoms: The system fails to recognize PDF and other acceptable file formats. Upload attempts result in errors or the files not being uploaded. Cause: The issue may be caused by a corrupted cache folder for EXP called “Documentum” in the user’s profile folder. Resolution: Step 1: Identify the Issue Attempt to upload a PDF or other acceptable formats to EXP/Kofax. If the file is not recognized, proceed to the next step. Step 2: Delete the Documentum Folder Navigate to the user’s profile folder: C:\Users\userid\Documentum . Ensure all browsers are closed to avoid the folder being in use. Delete the “Documentum” folder. Try uploading the file again to EXP/Kofax. Step 3: Upload from Original Source Path If deleting the Documentum folder does not resolve the issue, try uploading the file from its original source path. Avoid uploading from Recent Items as it may create a .lnk file instead. Workaround: If the full resolution is not immediately available, try the following temporary solutions: Restart the computer and attempt the upload again. Use a different browser or computer to upload the file. Additional Notes: Ensure that all browsers are closed before deleting the Documentum folder to avoid any “in-use” errors. Related articles: EXP/Kofax documentation for acceptable file formats. re it’s located. If you try to upload the file from Recent Items it will create a .lnk and upload that instead.
